questions.questionText: A cuboid has dimensions 5 cm, 8 cm, and 10 cm. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cuboid = length × width × height. So, 5 cm × 8 cm × 10 cm = 400 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cylinder has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 7 cm. Calculate its volume. Use $\pi$ = 3.14.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = $\pi \times r^2$ × h. So, 3.14 × $(3 cm)^2$ × 7 cm = 3.14 × 9 $\text{cm}^2$ × 7 cm = 197.82 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A sphere has a radius of 6 cm. Calculate its volume, leaving your answer in terms $of \pi .$
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = ($\frac{4}{3}$) × $\pi \times r^3$. So, ($\frac{4}{3}$) × $\pi \times (6 cm)^3$ = ($\frac{4}{3}$) × $\pi$ × 216 $\text{cm}^3$ = $288\pi \text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A square-based pyramid has a base side length of 6 cm and a perpendicular height of 10 cm. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a pyramid = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × base area × height. Base area = 6 cm × 6 cm = 36 $\text{cm}^2$. So, ($\frac{1}{3}$) × 36 $\text{cm}^2$ × 10 cm = 120 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cone has a radius of 5 cm and a height of 12 cm. Calculate its volume, leaving your answer in terms $of \pi .$
questions.explanation: Volume of a cone = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × $\pi \times r^2$ × h. So, ($\frac{1}{3}$) × $\pi \times (5 cm)^2$ × 12 cm = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × $\pi$ × 25 $\text{cm}^2$ × 12 cm = $100\pi \text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cuboid has a volume of 240 $\text{cm}^3$. If its length is 8 cm and width is 5 cm, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height. So, 240 $\text{cm}^3$ = 8 cm × 5 cm × height. 240 = 40 × height. Height = $\frac{240}{40}$ = 6 cm.

questions.questionText: The volume of a cylinder is $300\pi \text{cm}^3$. If its height is 12 cm, what is its radius?
A hemispherical bowl has a radius of 9 cm. Calculate the volume of the bowl, leaving your answer in terms $of \pi .$
questions.explanation: Volume of a hemisphere = ($\frac{1}{2}$) × ($\frac{4}{3}$) × $\pi \times r^3$ = ($\frac{2}{3}$) × $\pi \times r^3$. So, ($\frac{2}{3}$) × $\pi \times (9 cm)^3$ = ($\frac{2}{3}$) × $\pi$ × 729 $\text{cm}^3$ = $486\pi \text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A prism has a uniform cross-section with an area of 45 $\text{cm}^2$. If the length of the prism is 10 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = cross-sectional area × length. So, 45 $\text{cm}^2$ × 10 cm = 450 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A rectangular-based pyramid has a base measuring 4 cm by 7 cm and a volume of 84 $\text{cm}^3$. What is the perpendicular height of the pyramid?
questions.explanation: Volume of a pyramid = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × base area × height. Base area = 4 cm × 7 cm = 28 $\text{cm}^2$. So, 84 $\text{cm}^3$ = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × 28 $\text{cm}^2$ × height. 84 = ($\frac{28}{3}$) × height. Height = (84 × 3) / 28 = 9 cm.

questions.questionText: Two geometrically similar solids have volumes in the ratio 8:27. What is the ratio of their corresponding lengths?
questions.explanation: For similar solids, the ratio of volumes is the cube of the ratio of lengths. If V₁:V₂ = L₁³:L₂³, then L₁:L₂ = ³$\sqrt{V}$₁:³$\sqrt{V}$₂. So, $\sqrt[3]{8}$:$\sqrt[3]{27}$ = 2:3.

questions.questionText: A cone has a volume of $75\pi \text{cm}^3$. If its radius is 5 cm, what is its height?
